title: Add finalizer for MariaDB resource cleanup on Keystone CR deletion
description:
# GitHub Issue #133: Add finalizer for MariaDB resource cleanup on Keystone CR deletion

**Category**: improvement | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: The controller does not use a finalizer. MariaDB Database, User, and Grant CRs created by reconcileDatabase do not have owner references to the Keystone CR. Add a finalizer that cleans up these MariaDB resources when the Keystone CR is deleted, preventing orphaned database resources.

**Motivation**: Without a finalizer, deleting a Keystone CR leaves MariaDB Database/User/Grant CRs behind. These orphaned resources consume MariaDB server capacity and create confusion during redeployment.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Keystone CR has a finalizer added during first reconcile
- [ ] On deletion, the finalizer handler deletes MariaDB Database, User, and Grant CRs
- [ ] Finalizer is removed after cleanup completes
- [ ] E2E test validates clean deletion of all MariaDB resources
- [ ] Graceful handling when MariaDB resources are already gone

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/133

stories.title: Cluster admin deletes a Keystone CR without leaving MariaDB resources behind
stories.want: deleting a Keystone CR to automatically remove the associated MariaDB Database, User, and Grant CRs
stories.so_that: I do not have to manually clean up orphaned MariaDB resources after teardown or redeployment (CC-0078)
criteria: After kubectl delete keystone <name>, the MariaDB Database CR of the same name/namespace is deleted (NotFound)
After kubectl delete keystone <name>, the MariaDB User CR of the same name/namespace is deleted (NotFound)
After kubectl delete keystone <name>, the MariaDB Grant CR of the same name/namespace is deleted (NotFound)
The Keystone CR itself is removed from etcd only after all three MariaDB CRs are gone
Deletion never blocks indefinitely — the finalizer is removed and the CR disappears within a bounded time window

stories.title: Operator developer can trace finalizer lifecycle via conditions and events
stories.want: the controller to set a finalizer on first reconcile and emit an Event when cleanup starts and completes
stories.so_that: I can observe and debug finalizer behaviour without attaching a debugger (CC-0078)
criteria: On first reconcile of a Keystone CR, metadata.finalizers contains "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/finalizer" after the reconcile call returns
When a Keystone CR enters Terminating state, a Normal Event with reason "FinalizingDatabase" is emitted
When finalizer cleanup completes and the finalizer is removed, a Normal Event with reason "DatabaseFinalized" is emitted
All finalizer-related log lines include the Keystone name and namespace

stories.title: Brownfield Keystone (Host-based) deletion is not blocked by the finalizer
stories.want: Keystone CRs that use Spec.Database.Host (no ClusterRef) to be deleted without attempting MariaDB CR cleanup
stories.so_that: existing brownfield deployments are not broken by finalizer logic that does not apply to them (CC-0078)
criteria: A Keystone CR with only Spec.Database.Host set still has the finalizer added on first reconcile
On deletion, the finalizer handler does not issue Delete calls against MariaDB CRs because none were created
Finalizer is removed and the CR is cleaned up within the same reconcile pass that observes deletion
No spurious NotFound error events are emitted in brownfield mode

stories.title: Finalizer handler tolerates already-deleted MariaDB resources
stories.want: finalizer cleanup to succeed when Database/User/Grant CRs have already been garbage-collected or deleted by another actor
stories.so_that: re-running the finalizer (after operator restart, retry, or external deletion) never blocks CR removal (CC-0078)
criteria: If the Database CR is already NotFound, the delete call is treated as success
If the User CR is already NotFound, the delete call is treated as success
If the Grant CR is already NotFound, the delete call is treated as success
Reconcile returns no error and proceeds to remove the finalizer when all three CRs are absent
The finalizer handler is idempotent: calling it twice in a row produces the same outcome

stories.title: Existing Keystone CRs without finalizer are upgraded transparently
stories.want: Keystone CRs created before this change to gain the finalizer on their next reconcile
stories.so_that: upgrading the operator does not require manual migration of existing CRs (CC-0078)
criteria: A CR reconciled by the old controller version (no finalizer) receives the finalizer on its first reconcile under the new version
The finalizer addition triggers a single extra reconcile (requeue) and then proceeds normally
The CR's Status and Conditions are unchanged by the finalizer addition itself
Deletion of such an upgraded CR triggers the full cleanup flow

stories.title: E2E user validates deterministic deletion cleanup in chainsaw tests
stories.want: the chainsaw deletion-cleanup test to assert both the finalizer presence and the eventual absence of all MariaDB CRs
stories.so_that: regressions in finalizer behaviour are caught in CI before release (CC-0078)
criteria: tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml asserts metadata.finalizers contains the expected finalizer name after Ready=True
After CR deletion, the test asserts Database, User, and Grant CRs are NotFound
The deletion step uses a bounded timeout (<= 2 minutes) and fails if the CR remains in Terminating state
No changes to the existing Ready-gate assertions are required for the test to pass

stories.title: Documentation reader understands the finalizer contract
stories.want: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md to describe the finalizer name, lifecycle, cleanup scope, and failure modes
stories.so_that: future contributors and SREs can reason about teardown behaviour without reading the controller source (CC-0078)
criteria: Reference doc lists the finalizer constant name and the resources it covers
Reference doc describes the reconcile branching for DeletionTimestamp.IsZero()
Reference doc documents graceful NotFound handling and idempotency
Reference doc notes that brownfield (Host-only) CRs still receive the finalizer but perform no-op cleanup

requirements.description: The KeystoneReconciler SHALL add a finalizer named "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/finalizer" to every Keystone CR on its first reconcile when the CR is not being deleted (CC-0078)
requirements.rationale: The finalizer is the hook that guarantees ordered cleanup of MariaDB Database/User/Grant CRs before the Keystone CR disappears from etcd

scenarios.name: New CR gains finalizer
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR without the finalizer is reconciled and DeletionTimestamp is zero
scenarios.then: the controller appends the finalizer via controllerutil.AddFinalizer and Update, then requeues
and_then: the CR observed after the reconcile has metadata.finalizers containing the finalizer name
no sub-reconciler is invoked on this initial pass once the finalizer has been patched

scenarios.name: CR with finalizer is reconciled normally
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR already containing the finalizer is reconciled and DeletionTimestamp is zero
scenarios.then: the controller does not modify metadata.finalizers and proceeds to the sub-reconciler pipeline
and_then: no extra Update call is issued when the finalizer is already present

scenarios.name: Update conflict is surfaced for retry
scenarios.when: the finalizer Update call returns a Conflict error due to stale resource version
scenarios.then: the controller returns the error so controller-runtime requeues with backoff
and_then: no partial state (e.g. finalizer half-written) is persisted
no Event is emitted for this transient error to avoid event spam

requirements.description: When a Keystone CR is being deleted (metadata.DeletionTimestamp non-zero) and the finalizer is present, the controller SHALL delete the MariaDB Database, User, and Grant CRs named after the Keystone CR in the same namespace before removing the finalizer (CC-0078)
requirements.rationale: Deterministic cleanup of MariaDB resources prevents orphaned DB/User/Grant CRs that consume MariaDB server capacity and create confusion on redeployment

scenarios.name: All three MariaDB CRs deleted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR with ClusterRef set enters Terminating state with existing Database, User, Grant CRs
scenarios.then: the finalizer handler issues Delete for each CR and returns a requeue until all three are gone
and_then: once all three are NotFound, the handler removes the finalizer via controllerutil.RemoveFinalizer and Update
a Normal Event with reason "DatabaseFinalized" is recorded on the Keystone CR before the Update

scenarios.name: Brownfield CR is finalized without deleting MariaDB CRs
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR with Spec.Database.Host set and no ClusterRef enters Terminating state
scenarios.then: the finalizer handler still issues idempotent Delete-or-NotFound checks and removes the finalizer in the same reconcile pass
and_then: no error is returned even though no MariaDB CR was ever created by this operator
a Normal Event with reason "DatabaseFinalized" is still emitted for observability

scenarios.name: CR without finalizer is deleted without running cleanup
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR that never had the finalizer (e.g., created under an older controller version or forcibly patched) enters Terminating state
scenarios.then: the controller returns ctrl.Result{} without attempting MariaDB CR cleanup and without removing a finalizer it does not own
and_then: the CR is immediately garbage-collected by the API server
no Event is emitted

requirements.description: The finalizer handler SHALL treat apierrors.IsNotFound responses from Delete and Get calls as success and SHALL NOT propagate them as reconciler errors (CC-0078)
requirements.rationale: The MariaDB CRs may already be gone (external deletion, completed garbage collection, or a previous finalizer pass) and must not block Keystone CR removal

scenarios.name: Database already gone
scenarios.when: the finalizer issues Delete on the Database CR and receives NotFound
scenarios.then: the handler proceeds to the User CR without returning an error
and_then: no error Event is emitted
the log records the skipped delete at V(1) debug level only

scenarios.name: User already gone mid-flight
scenarios.when: Database delete succeeds but the User CR was deleted externally between reconcile calls
scenarios.then: the User Delete returns NotFound and the handler proceeds to the Grant CR

scenarios.name: All three already gone
scenarios.when: the finalizer handler is re-invoked after a successful previous cleanup and all three CRs are NotFound
scenarios.then: the handler removes the finalizer and returns ctrl.Result{} without error
and_then: one Normal Event with reason "DatabaseFinalized" is still emitted to mark completion

requirements.description: While any of the MariaDB Database/User/Grant CRs still exist after a Delete has been issued, the finalizer handler SHALL requeue using RequeueDatabaseWait and SHALL NOT remove the finalizer (CC-0078)
requirements.rationale: The MariaDB operator owns the deletion of its CRs; the Keystone operator must wait for actual removal before declaring cleanup complete

scenarios.name: Database still present after Delete
scenarios.when: Delete on the Database CR succeeds but a subsequent Get still returns the CR (DeletionTimestamp set)
scenarios.then: the handler returns ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: RequeueDatabaseWait}, nil
and_then: the finalizer is left on the Keystone CR so the next reconcile re-enters cleanup
log line "waiting for MariaDB resources to be deleted" is recorded at Info level

scenarios.name: User still present after Delete
scenarios.when: Database is gone but User CR still exists (Terminating)
scenarios.then: the handler returns a RequeueAfter without removing the finalizer

scenarios.name: Grant still present after Delete
scenarios.when: Database and User are gone but Grant CR still exists (Terminating)
scenarios.then: the handler returns a RequeueAfter without removing the finalizer

requirements.description: The finalizer constant SHALL be defined as an exported or package-level string in operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go with the value "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/finalizer" (CC-0078)
requirements.rationale: Using a single constant prevents drift between Reconcile, the finalizer handler, unit tests, and documentation

scenarios.name: Constant defined once
scenarios.when: the codebase is grepped for the finalizer name
scenarios.then: the literal string appears at most twice: the constant declaration and docstring examples
and_then: no duplicate finalizer identifiers exist in controller or test code

scenarios.name: Constant uses canonical Kubernetes API group prefix
scenarios.when: the finalizer name is validated against the Keystone CRD's group "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io"
scenarios.then: the finalizer string starts with that group prefix
and_then: kubectl get keystone -o yaml displays the finalizer under the canonical group

scenarios.name: Constant is referenced from tests
scenarios.when: unit and integration tests assert finalizer presence
scenarios.then: they import and compare against the exported constant rather than a string literal

requirements.description: The existing Reconcile entry point SHALL be updated so that finalizer handling executes after the CR fetch and before any sub-reconciler runs (CC-0078)
requirements.rationale: Running sub-reconcilers on a Terminating CR would create MariaDB resources that the finalizer is about to delete, causing an infinite re-creation loop

scenarios.name: Terminating CR skips sub-reconcilers
scenarios.when: Reconcile observes a CR with DeletionTimestamp non-zero
scenarios.then: reconcileSecrets, reconcileConfig, reconcileDatabase, and all subsequent sub-reconcilers are NOT invoked
and_then: control flow goes directly to the finalizer handler

scenarios.name: Non-terminating CR without finalizer triggers requeue
scenarios.when: Reconcile observes a live CR missing the finalizer
scenarios.then: the finalizer is added and the function returns a requeue without running sub-reconcilers in the same pass
and_then: the next reconcile sees the finalizer already present and proceeds normally

scenarios.name: Live CR with finalizer runs the normal pipeline
scenarios.when: Reconcile observes a live CR that already contains the finalizer
scenarios.then: the existing sub-reconciler pipeline executes unchanged
and_then: no behavioural regression in the happy path is introduced

requirements.description: The controller SHALL emit Kubernetes Events on finalizer lifecycle transitions: Normal "FinalizingDatabase" when cleanup starts, Normal "DatabaseFinalized" when it completes (CC-0078)
requirements.rationale: Operators rely on kubectl describe keystone to understand why a CR is in Terminating state and how long cleanup is taking

scenarios.name: Start event on first terminating reconcile
scenarios.when: the controller first observes DeletionTimestamp non-zero on a CR with the finalizer
scenarios.then: a Normal Event with reason "FinalizingDatabase" and a message mentioning the CR name is recorded
and_then: subsequent reconciles during the same termination do NOT re-emit the start event

scenarios.name: Completion event on finalizer removal
scenarios.when: all MariaDB CRs are confirmed absent and the controller is about to remove the finalizer
scenarios.then: a Normal Event with reason "DatabaseFinalized" and a summary of deleted resources is recorded before the Update
and_then: the event contains at minimum the Keystone CR name

scenarios.name: No event spam on transient errors
scenarios.when: a transient error (e.g. throttling) occurs during cleanup and the reconcile retries
scenarios.then: no additional Events are emitted for the retry path
and_then: only the start and completion events appear in kubectl describe output

requirements.description: The E2E chainsaw test tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml SHALL assert finalizer presence after Ready=True and MariaDB CR absence after deletion within 2 minutes (CC-0078)
requirements.rationale: Regressions in finalizer behaviour must be caught in CI before release; the existing cleanup test already covers owner-ref GC but does not exercise the finalizer contract explicitly

scenarios.name: Finalizer asserted on Ready CR
scenarios.when: the test step that waits for Ready=True asserts metadata.finalizers
scenarios.then: the assertion passes when the finalizer "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/finalizer" is present

scenarios.name: Deletion completes within bounded time
scenarios.when: the test deletes the Keystone CR and waits for MariaDB CRs to disappear
scenarios.then: all three (Database, User, Grant) are NotFound within a 2-minute timeout
and_then: the Keystone CR itself is NotFound after deletion

scenarios.name: Test fails fast if finalizer stuck
scenarios.when: the finalizer is never removed and the Keystone CR remains in Terminating state past the timeout
scenarios.then: chainsaw reports a failure with a descriptive message including the stuck finalizer name

requirements.description: The reference documentation (docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md) SHALL describe the finalizer name, lifecycle, resources covered, and idempotency guarantees (CC-0078)
requirements.rationale: Reference docs are the Diataxis quadrant for information-oriented content; finalizer behaviour is part of the controller's public contract and must be documented

scenarios.name: Reference doc lists finalizer name
scenarios.when: a reader searches docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md for "finalizer"
scenarios.then: they find the constant value "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/finalizer" and the file path where it is defined

scenarios.name: Reference doc describes cleanup scope
scenarios.when: a reader asks which resources the finalizer deletes
scenarios.then: the doc names MariaDB Database, User, and Grant CRs matching keystone.metadata.name in the same namespace

scenarios.name: Reference doc notes brownfield no-op
scenarios.when: a reader looks up behaviour for Host-based (brownfield) CRs
scenarios.then: the doc explicitly states the finalizer is still added but cleanup is a no-op (graceful NotFound)

tasks.title: Define package-level constant keystoneFinalizer = "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/finalizer" in operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go and unit test asserting its value (REQ-005, CC-0078)

Implement finalizeDatabaseResources(ctx, keystone) in operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go: issue client.Delete (IgnoreNotFound) for Database, User, Grant CRs named after keystone.Name in keystone.Namespace; re-Get each to detect in-flight terminations; return (done bool, err error) where done=true only when all three are confirmed NotFound (REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, CC-0078)

Add unit tests for finalizeDatabaseResources in operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database_test.go covering: all three CRs present (done=false after Delete, done=true on re-run when absent), NotFound tolerated per-resource, brownfield no-op, idempotency, per-scenario error propagation; use existing dbTestScheme and managedKeystone/brownfieldKeystone helpers (REQ-002 to REQ-004, CC-0078)

Modify Reconcile in operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go: after the CR Get, branch on !keystone.DeletionTimestamp.IsZero(); if terminating and finalizer present, emit Normal "FinalizingDatabase" event once, call finalizeDatabaseResources, on done=false return ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: RequeueDatabaseWait}, on done=true emit Normal "DatabaseFinalized", RemoveFinalizer, Update, return; if terminating without finalizer, return empty result (REQ-002, REQ-006, REQ-007, CC-0078)

In the same Reconcile function, add a pre-sub-reconciler block that calls controllerutil.AddFinalizer when missing (live CR path) and returns ctrl.Result{Requeue: true} after Update so the next pass runs with the finalizer already persisted (REQ-001, REQ-006, CC-0078)

Use deduplication of the "FinalizingDatabase" event: only emit it when the CR does NOT already have a condition/annotation marker or when no such event has been emitted this termination; simplest implementation uses a single emission at the first terminating observation by checking a sentinel (e.g., that all three MariaDB CRs still exist or their DeletionTimestamps are zero) (REQ-007, CC-0078)

Add unit tests in operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go: TestReconcile_AddsFinalizerOnFirstReconcile, TestReconcile_FinalizerAlreadyPresent_NoExtraUpdate, TestReconcile_FinalizerUpdateConflict_ReturnsError (use fake client interceptor to inject Conflict), TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_SkipsSubReconcilers, TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_RequeuesWhileMariaDBResourcesExist, TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_WithoutFinalizer_NoOp (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-004, REQ-006, CC-0078)

Add unit tests for event emission in operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go: TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_EmitsFinalizingDatabaseEvent, TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_EmitsDatabaseFinalizedEvent, using record.FakeRecorder with a buffered channel to capture Events (REQ-007, CC-0078)

Add integration test TestIntegration_FinalizerLifecycle_AddAndRemove in operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go using envtest: create managed-mode Keystone CR, Eventually assert finalizer present; seed Database/User/Grant; delete Keystone; Eventually assert MariaDB CRs are NotFound and the Keystone CR itself is NotFound (REQ-002, CC-0078)

Add integration test TestIntegration_FinalizerBrownfieldDeletion in operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go: create brownfield (Host-only) Keystone CR, assert finalizer present, delete CR, assert clean removal without any MariaDB CR operations (REQ-002, CC-0078)

Extend tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml: add an assertion inside the existing Ready=True step that metadata.finalizers contains "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/finalizer"; keep the existing Database/User/Grant NotFound error assertions; ensure delete step uses a bounded timeout (<= 2m) (REQ-008, CC-0078)

Update docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md: add a "Finalizer" subsection under Controller Registration or a new top-level section; document finalizer constant name, resources covered (Database, User, Grant in same namespace named after the CR), reconcile branching on DeletionTimestamp, NotFound tolerance, idempotency, brownfield no-op behaviour, and the Events emitted (REQ-009, CC-0078)

test_specifications.story: Operator developer can trace finalizer lifecycle via conditions and events
test_specifications.expected: Reconcile on a Keystone CR without the finalizer results in metadata.finalizers containing the finalizer constant and a requeue is returned

test_specifications.story: Existing Keystone CRs without finalizer are upgraded transparently
test_specifications.expected: Reconcile on a CR that already has the finalizer does not issue an extra Update for finalizer maintenance and proceeds to the sub-reconciler pipeline

test_specifications.story: Operator developer can trace finalizer lifecycle via conditions and events
test_specifications.expected: When the fake client is configured to return a Conflict on Update, Reconcile returns the error unchanged so controller-runtime retries

test_specifications.story: Cluster admin deletes a Keystone CR without leaving MariaDB resources behind
test_specifications.expected: finalizeDatabaseResources issues Delete for Database, User, and Grant CRs and returns done=true when all three are NotFound on the next Get

test_specifications.story: Brownfield Keystone (Host-based) deletion is not blocked by the finalizer
test_specifications.expected: When Spec.Database.ClusterRef is nil and no MariaDB CRs exist, finalizeDatabaseResources returns done=true, nil on the first call

test_specifications.story: Finalizer handler tolerates already-deleted MariaDB resources
test_specifications.expected: Each of Database/User/Grant separately absent results in no error and proceeds to the next resource

test_specifications.story: Finalizer handler tolerates already-deleted MariaDB resources
test_specifications.expected: Calling finalizeDatabaseResources twice in a row with no MariaDB CRs present returns done=true, nil both times without additional Delete calls on the second invocation

test_specifications.story: Cluster admin deletes a Keystone CR without leaving MariaDB resources behind
test_specifications.expected: When Database/User/Grant CRs still exist after Delete (simulated by leaving them in the fake client store), finalizeDatabaseResources returns done=false and no error

test_specifications.story: Cluster admin deletes a Keystone CR without leaving MariaDB resources behind
test_specifications.expected: Reconcile on a Terminating CR with existing MariaDB CRs returns ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: RequeueDatabaseWait} and does NOT remove the finalizer

test_specifications.story: Documentation reader understands the finalizer contract
test_specifications.expected: The package-level finalizer constant equals "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/finalizer" and is the only definition of that string in the package

test_specifications.story: Cluster admin deletes a Keystone CR without leaving MariaDB resources behind
test_specifications.expected: Reconcile on a Terminating CR never invokes reconcileSecrets/reconcileConfig/etc.; asserted by observing that no Secret/ConfigMap/Job is created during the call

test_specifications.story: Existing Keystone CRs without finalizer are upgraded transparently
test_specifications.expected: A Terminating CR that never had the finalizer returns ctrl.Result{}, nil with no side effects (no Delete calls, no Events)

test_specifications.story: Operator developer can trace finalizer lifecycle via conditions and events
test_specifications.expected: When cleanup completes, a Normal Event with reason "DatabaseFinalized" is recorded on the fake record.EventRecorder

test_specifications.story: Operator developer can trace finalizer lifecycle via conditions and events
test_specifications.expected: On the first reconcile observing DeletionTimestamp, a Normal Event with reason "FinalizingDatabase" is recorded

test_specifications.story: Cluster admin deletes a Keystone CR without leaving MariaDB resources behind
test_specifications.expected: Under envtest, creating a Keystone CR results in the finalizer being added; deleting it triggers MariaDB CR deletion and finalizer removal; the CR is eventually NotFound

test_specifications.story: Brownfield Keystone (Host-based) deletion is not blocked by the finalizer
test_specifications.expected: A brownfield Keystone CR is deleted cleanly via the finalizer path with no MariaDB CR operations

test_specifications.story: E2E user validates deterministic deletion cleanup in chainsaw tests
test_specifications.expected: After the Ready=True assertion, the test asserts metadata.finalizers contains "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/finalizer"

test_specifications.story: E2E user validates deterministic deletion cleanup in chainsaw tests
test_specifications.expected: After deletion, Database, User, and Grant CRs are NotFound within a 2-minute bounded timeout; the Keystone CR is also NotFound

review_criteria: BLOCKER: Reconcile correctly branches on DeletionTimestamp before invoking any sub-reconciler (REQ-006); verified by TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_SkipsSubReconcilers
BLOCKER: The finalizer constant is defined once and uses value "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/finalizer" (REQ-005); grep confirms no duplicate string literals

BLOCKER: finalizeDatabaseResources returns done=true only when all three MariaDB CRs are confirmed NotFound on a fresh Get after Delete (REQ-002, REQ-004); NotFound from any Delete/Get is treated as success (REQ-003)
BLOCKER: Finalizer is removed via controllerutil.RemoveFinalizer + client.Update only after done=true (REQ-002)
BLOCKER: All new unit tests, integration tests, and the E2E test for CC-0078 pass in CI; go vet and golangci-lint produce no new findings
CRITICAL: CRs without the finalizer are not blocked on deletion (REQ-002); TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_WithoutFinalizer_NoOp passes

CRITICAL: Brownfield (Host-only) CRs still receive the finalizer but the cleanup is a no-op (REQ-002); TestFinalizeDatabaseResources_BrownfieldIsNoop and TestIntegration_FinalizerBrownfieldDeletion pass
CRITICAL: Events "FinalizingDatabase" (start) and "DatabaseFinalized" (completion) are emitted exactly once each per deletion (REQ-007); confirmed by unit tests using record.FakeRecorder
CRITICAL: Integration tests use envtest and the existing testutil helpers; they do not fabricate their own scheme or bypass the shared MariaDB simulator pattern
MINOR: Reference docs in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md are updated (REQ-009) and link to the controller source via the file path used in the constant declaration
MINOR: Every new log line, event, and doc reference includes the feature ID "CC-0078"
MINOR: No unrelated refactoring of existing reconcileDatabase logic; task scope strictly limited to finalizer handling
implementation_notes:
ARCHITECTURAL DECISIONS (CC-0078):

1. Finalizer name: "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/finalizer". Uses the canonical CRD group prefix so it is unambiguous under kubectl get keystone -o yaml. Defined once as a package-level constant (keystoneFinalizer) in keystone_controller.go.

2. Reconcile branching: the finalizer block executes immediately after the CR Get and before any sub-reconciler. This prevents a Terminating CR from recreating the MariaDB CRs that the finalizer is about to delete (would cause an infinite loop).

3. Cleanup strategy: explicit Delete + re-Get rather than relying solely on owner-ref garbage collection. Owner references (already set via controllerutil.SetControllerReference in internal/common/database/database.go) would normally suffice, but a finalizer gives deterministic, observable cleanup with events and log lines. The two mechanisms are complementary and do not conflict: Delete is idempotent under NotFound.

4. done=true vs done=false: finalizeDatabaseResources returns done=true only after a fresh Get confirms NotFound on all three resources. This distinguishes "just issued Delete, CR still Terminating" from "CR is fully gone" and prevents premature finalizer removal (which would strand CRs in a deleting state on the API server).

5. Brownfield (Host-only) CRs also receive the finalizer. The cleanup path calls Delete on the name-matched Database/User/Grant and tolerates NotFound, so brownfield CRs finish cleanup in a single reconcile pass. This avoids a conditional on Spec.Database.ClusterRef in the finalizer logic and keeps the code branch-free.

6. Event deduplication: "FinalizingDatabase" is emitted only on the first terminating reconcile. Determined by checking whether any of the three MariaDB CRs still exist before Delete — if all three are already absent on entry, skip the start event and emit only the completion event. This avoids event spam under requeue loops.

7. Log correlation: every finalizer-related log line includes keystone name/namespace via log.FromContext(ctx).WithValues(...). Follows the established "include-correlation-fields-in-operator-log-entries" review pattern at .planwerk/review_patterns/.

DATA MODEL:
- No schema changes. metadata.finalizers is a built-in Kubernetes array on every object.
- Constant: keystoneFinalizer string = "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/finalizer".

ARCHITECTURE DIAGRAM:

  +------------------+       +---------------------------+
  |  kubectl delete  | ----> |  Keystone CR              |
  |  keystone <name> |       |  (DeletionTimestamp set)  |
  +------------------+       +---------------------------+
                                          |
                                          v
                             +---------------------------+
                             |  KeystoneReconciler.      |
                             |  Reconcile()              |
                             |   └─ if DeletionTS != 0 &&|
                             |      finalizer present     |
                             +---------------------------+
                                          |
                                          v
                             +---------------------------+
                             |  finalizeDatabaseResources|
                             |   ─ Delete(Database) ----+--> MariaDB operator deletes DB
                             |   ─ Delete(User)     ----+--> MariaDB operator deletes User
                             |   ─ Delete(Grant)    ----+--> MariaDB operator deletes Grant
                             |   ─ re-Get all three     |
                             +---------------------------+
                                          |
                             all three NotFound?
                                  yes /          \ no
                                     v            v
                     RemoveFinalizer +Update    RequeueDatabaseWait
                                     |
                                     v
                             API server deletes Keystone CR

DATA FLOW:
  1. User issues kubectl delete keystone <name>.
  2. API server sets DeletionTimestamp; Keystone CR stays in etcd because finalizer is present.
  3. Watch triggers Reconcile.
  4. Reconcile Gets the CR, sees DeletionTimestamp != 0, skips sub-reconcilers.
  5. On first pass: emit "FinalizingDatabase" event.
  6. Call finalizeDatabaseResources: Delete(IgnoreNotFound) on each of Database/User/Grant.
  7. Re-Get each: if any still present (Terminating), requeue after RequeueDatabaseWait.
  8. Next reconcile: same cleanup call; if all NotFound, emit "DatabaseFinalized", RemoveFinalizer, Update.
  9. API server observes empty finalizers list and garbage-collects the Keystone CR.

PITFALLS TO AVOID:
- Do NOT remove the finalizer before confirming NotFound on all three resources — would leave orphaned Terminating CRs.
- Do NOT emit "FinalizingDatabase" on every requeue — check whether any resource still exists on entry to emit exactly once.
- Do NOT run sub-reconcilers on a Terminating CR — they would recreate the MariaDB CRs, causing an infinite loop.
- Do NOT add Update conflict error events — controller-runtime handles retry, events would be noise.
- Keep the finalizer constant private to the package (lowercase initial) unless another package genuinely needs it; exporting creates a larger API surface.

PATTERNS TO FOLLOW:
- Reconcile structure and updateStatus usage already defined in keystone_controller.go:79-185.
- Fake client unit tests with testScheme(), managedKeystone(), record.NewFakeRecorder() from existing test files (keystone_controller_test.go, reconcile_database_test.go).
- Integration tests follow the pattern in integration_test.go (envtest, Gomega Eventually, testutil.SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController).
- Chainsaw test steps follow the structure in tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml (apply, assert, delete, error).
- Diataxis Reference docs at docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md; existing "Finalizers" one-liner at architecture/docs/09-implementation/04-keystone-reconciler.md:500 is the authoritative architectural statement and is already consistent with this implementation.
